standards.title: Governance and Leadership Framework
standards.description: The hospital has a well-defined governance structure with clear leadership accountability for organizational direction, quality, and patient safety.

objective_elements.description: A governing body or board is formally constituted with documented authority, composition, and defined responsibilities for hospital oversight.

measurable_elements.description: The governing body charter or bylaws are documented, specifying membership composition, term of office, meeting frequency, and quorum requirements.

objective_elements.description: The governing body appoints qualified senior leadership and defines their authority, responsibilities, and accountability mechanisms.

measurable_elements.description: Appointment letters and job descriptions for senior leadership positions clearly delineate authority, roles, and accountability lines.

objective_elements.description: The governing body regularly reviews and approves the hospital's mission, vision, and values, ensuring alignment with quality and safety goals.

measurable_elements.description: Board meeting minutes reflect periodic review and endorsement of the organization's mission, vision, values, and strategic priorities.

objective_elements.description: Senior leaders ensure that required statutory and regulatory licenses and permits are obtained, displayed, and kept current.

measurable_elements.description: A centralized register of all statutory licenses, registrations, and permits is maintained with renewal tracking and compliance verification.

objective_elements.description: Leadership communicates the organization's direction, priorities, and performance outcomes to all levels of staff.

measurable_elements.description: Documented communication channels exist (town halls, newsletters, intranet updates) for cascading organizational priorities and performance results to staff.

objective_elements.description: The governing body reviews key quality and safety indicators, patient feedback, and adverse event trends on a defined schedule.

measurable_elements.description: Board agendas and minutes demonstrate regular review of quality dashboards, safety metrics, patient satisfaction data, and sentinel event reports.

objective_elements.description: The organization has mechanisms for leadership succession planning and continuity of administrative functions.

measurable_elements.description: A documented succession plan exists for key leadership positions, identifying potential successors and development pathways.

objective_elements.description: Leadership fosters a culture of continuous improvement through benchmarking against peer organizations and best practice adoption.

measurable_elements.description: Evidence of benchmarking activities comparing key performance indicators with peer institutions and documented plans for adopting identified best practices.

standards.title: Ethics and Transparency in Hospital Operations
standards.description: The organization operates with a clearly defined ethical framework that promotes transparency, accountability, and integrity in all activities.

objective_elements.description: A documented code of ethics and professional conduct is established, communicated to all staff, and enforced through appropriate mechanisms.

measurable_elements.description: A written code of ethics covering conflicts of interest, professional conduct, anti-bribery, and ethical decision-making is approved by the governing body and distributed to all employees.

objective_elements.description: An ethics committee or equivalent body is constituted to address ethical dilemmas in clinical care, research, and organizational operations.

measurable_elements.description: The ethics committee has documented terms of reference, multi-disciplinary membership, and maintains minutes of meetings with resolution tracking for ethical issues raised.

objective_elements.description: The organization maintains transparent financial practices with disclosed fee structures, billing processes, and audit mechanisms.

measurable_elements.description: Rate lists, package pricing, and billing policies are publicly displayed and regularly reviewed to ensure accuracy and transparency for patients and families.

objective_elements.description: A mechanism for reporting unethical behavior or compliance violations exists, with safeguards to protect whistleblowers.

measurable_elements.description: A documented whistleblower policy with anonymous reporting channels, investigation procedures, and non-retaliation protections is implemented and communicated to staff.

objective_elements.description: Ethical considerations in clinical research, organ donation, and end-of-life care are addressed through defined protocols.

measurable_elements.description: Documented protocols cover ethical review of clinical research proposals, organ transplant ethics, and advance directive/end-of-life decision frameworks.

objective_elements.description: The organization conducts periodic ethics audits and incorporates findings into governance improvement plans.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic ethics audit reports are produced, presented to leadership, and corrective action plans are tracked to closure with measurable outcomes.

standards.title: Organizational Structure and Accountability
standards.description: The organization maintains a clear structure defining roles, departments, and reporting relationships to ensure efficient operations and accountability.

objective_elements.description: An organizational chart is maintained reflecting current reporting relationships, departmental structure, and lines of authority across all functions.

measurable_elements.description: A current organizational chart is displayed and accessible, showing all departments, key positions, reporting lines, and committee structures.

objective_elements.description: Each department or service has a designated leader with defined responsibilities for service delivery, quality, and resource management.

measurable_elements.description: Each clinical and non-clinical department has a named head with a documented job description specifying quality oversight, budget management, and reporting obligations.

objective_elements.description: Committees and teams essential for quality, safety, infection control, and other key functions are formally constituted and operational.

measurable_elements.description: Terms of reference for all mandatory committees (quality, safety, infection control, pharmacy, medical records) are documented with member rosters, meeting schedules, and minutes.

objective_elements.description: Clear delegation of authority policies exist, defining who may make clinical, administrative, and financial decisions in the absence of primary decision-makers.

measurable_elements.description: A delegation of authority matrix is documented, specifying approval limits and decision-making powers for clinical, operational, and financial functions across hierarchy levels.

objective_elements.description: Inter-departmental coordination mechanisms are in place to ensure seamless patient care and operational efficiency.

measurable_elements.description: Documented inter-departmental coordination processes (regular coordination meetings, shared protocols, escalation pathways) ensure smooth workflow across clinical and support services.

objective_elements.description: The organization reviews and updates its structure periodically to align with changes in services, technology, or patient volume.

measurable_elements.description: Evidence of periodic reviews of organizational structure with documented rationale for any restructuring, role redefinition, or creation of new departments.

standards.title: Strategic and Operational Planning
standards.description: The organization undertakes systematic strategic and operational planning to set priorities, allocate resources, and achieve defined performance objectives.

objective_elements.description: A documented strategic plan covering a defined period is developed with input from clinical and administrative leaders, aligned with the mission and community needs.

measurable_elements.description: A board-approved strategic plan for a defined period (3-5 years) exists, developed through a consultative process involving clinical, operational, and financial stakeholders.

objective_elements.description: Annual operational plans and budgets are prepared in alignment with the strategic plan, with measurable targets for each department.

measurable_elements.description: Annual operational plans with department-level targets, timelines, resource allocation, and budget linkages are documented and approved by leadership.

objective_elements.description: Key performance indicators are defined for monitoring strategic plan implementation, with regular progress reviews by leadership.

measurable_elements.description: A defined set of KPIs aligned to strategic objectives is tracked through dashboards, with documented leadership reviews conducted at least quarterly.

objective_elements.description: Resource allocation decisions are guided by organizational priorities, patient needs analysis, and financial sustainability considerations.

measurable_elements.description: Documented evidence that capital and operational resource allocation decisions incorporate patient demand analysis, service priority ranking, and financial viability assessments.

objective_elements.description: The organization conducts periodic community health needs assessments to inform service planning and new program development.

measurable_elements.description: Community health needs assessments are conducted periodically, with findings documented and used as input for service expansion, new program design, or resource reallocation.

objective_elements.description: The organization actively pursues innovation, adopting new technologies, treatment modalities, or care delivery models based on evidence and strategic priorities.

measurable_elements.description: Documented innovation initiatives (new technology adoption, care model pilots, digital health integration) are linked to strategic goals with outcome evaluation reports.

standards.title: Risk Management and Compliance
standards.description: The organization has a comprehensive risk management framework that identifies, assesses, mitigates, and monitors risks across clinical, operational, and financial domains.

objective_elements.description: A documented organization-wide risk management plan is established, covering clinical risks, operational risks, financial risks, and reputational risks.

measurable_elements.description: A comprehensive risk management plan is documented, identifying risk categories, assessment methodology, risk registers, mitigation strategies, and monitoring schedules.

objective_elements.description: Risk identification and assessment processes are conducted periodically using standardized tools and involve relevant stakeholders from all departments.

measurable_elements.description: Risk assessments are performed at least annually using standardized tools (risk matrices, FMEA, or equivalent), with departmental participation and documented findings.

objective_elements.description: Risk mitigation plans are implemented with assigned owners, timelines, and monitoring mechanisms to track effectiveness of controls.

measurable_elements.description: Risk mitigation action plans with responsible owners, completion deadlines, and effectiveness metrics are documented in the risk register and reviewed in committee meetings.

objective_elements.description: The organization maintains compliance with all applicable regulatory and statutory requirements, with a documented compliance tracking system.

measurable_elements.description: A regulatory compliance tracker captures all applicable laws, regulations, and standards with status indicators, responsible parties, and renewal/audit dates.

objective_elements.description: Insurance and indemnity coverage is maintained for professional liability, property, and operational risks as applicable.

measurable_elements.description: Current insurance policies covering professional indemnity, public liability, property damage, and other operational risks are documented with renewal tracking.

objective_elements.description: The organization employs data-driven risk analytics and trending to predict and proactively address emerging risks.

measurable_elements.description: Risk trending reports using incident data, near-miss analysis, and predictive analytics are generated periodically and used for proactive risk reduction planning.

standards.title: Social Responsibility and Community Engagement
standards.description: The organization fulfills its social responsibilities by engaging with the community, promoting public health, and contributing to societal wellbeing.

objective_elements.description: The organization conducts community outreach programs focused on health promotion, disease prevention, and public health education.

measurable_elements.description: Documented community outreach activities (health camps, screening programs, awareness campaigns) are conducted with participation records, outcome reports, and annual planning.

objective_elements.description: The organization has policies and programs to address the healthcare needs of economically weaker sections and vulnerable populations.

measurable_elements.description: Documented policies for providing subsidized or free care to economically disadvantaged patients, with utilization records and annual reporting on beneficiaries served.

objective_elements.description: Environmental sustainability practices are adopted, including waste reduction, energy conservation, and green hospital initiatives.

measurable_elements.description: Environmental sustainability programs covering energy audit, water conservation, carbon footprint reduction, and green procurement practices are documented with measurable targets.

objective_elements.description: The organization supports education and training of healthcare professionals through teaching programs, internships, or academic collaborations.

measurable_elements.description: Academic affiliations, teaching programs, internship arrangements, or research collaborations are documented with agreements, participant records, and program outcome reports.

objective_elements.description: The organization demonstrates thought leadership in healthcare policy, contributing to regional or national health system improvement initiatives.

measurable_elements.description: Evidence of leadership participation in policy forums, healthcare improvement coalitions, published position papers, or advisory roles in national health programs.

standards.title: Fire Safety and Prevention Program
standards.description: The organization has a comprehensive fire safety program addressing prevention, detection, suppression, and safe evacuation of occupants.

objective_elements.description: The organization obtains and maintains a valid fire safety clearance certificate from the competent fire authority.

measurable_elements.description: A current, valid Fire No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the local fire authority is maintained and displayed, with renewal tracked prior to expiry.

objective_elements.description: Fire detection and alarm systems are installed throughout the facility, tested regularly, and maintained in working condition.

measurable_elements.description: Smoke detectors, heat sensors, and fire alarm systems are installed in all areas per national fire code, with documented quarterly testing and annual maintenance records.

objective_elements.description: Fire suppression equipment (extinguishers, sprinklers, hydrants) is available, accessible, properly maintained, and staff are trained in its use.

measurable_elements.description: Fire extinguishers of appropriate type and capacity are placed at designated locations, refilled/replaced per schedule, with monthly inspection checklists and annual hydrostatic testing records.

objective_elements.description: Fire evacuation plans and routes are displayed, exit routes are unobstructed, and emergency exits are clearly marked with illuminated signage.

measurable_elements.description: Floor-wise evacuation plans are displayed at strategic locations, exit routes are kept clear, emergency exit doors are functional, and illuminated exit signs are operational at all times.

objective_elements.description: Fire drills are conducted at defined intervals across all shifts, with participation records and post-drill evaluation reports.

measurable_elements.description: Fire drills are conducted at least twice annually covering all shifts, with attendance records, drill performance evaluations, and corrective actions documented for identified gaps.

objective_elements.description: All staff members receive fire safety training at induction and through periodic refresher programs covering fire response procedures.

measurable_elements.description: Fire safety training records demonstrate that all staff receive induction training and annual refresher training on fire prevention, alarm response, extinguisher use, and evacuation procedures.

objective_elements.description: Fire-resistant construction materials and fire compartmentalization are maintained per applicable building codes.

measurable_elements.description: Building fire safety assessments verify fire-rated doors, compartmentalization, fire-resistant materials in critical areas, and compliance with applicable building fire codes.

standards.title: Electrical Safety and Reliability
standards.description: The organization ensures safe, reliable electrical systems with appropriate safeguards, testing protocols, and emergency backup power.

objective_elements.description: Electrical installations comply with national electrical codes and are inspected periodically by qualified professionals.

measurable_elements.description: Electrical installation compliance certificates from licensed electrical inspectors are maintained, with periodic re-inspection per regulatory requirements.

objective_elements.description: Emergency power supply systems (generators, UPS) are available for critical areas and tested regularly to ensure reliable switchover.

measurable_elements.description: Emergency generators and UPS systems for ICU, OT, NICU, emergency, and other critical areas are tested weekly with load-testing logs and automatic transfer switch verification records.

objective_elements.description: Earthing and grounding systems are tested periodically, and surge protection devices are installed to safeguard sensitive equipment.

measurable_elements.description: Earthing resistance testing is conducted at least annually by qualified personnel, with documented results and corrective actions for any readings outside acceptable ranges.

objective_elements.description: Electrical safety signage, lockout/tagout procedures, and personal protective equipment are provided for maintenance personnel.

measurable_elements.description: Danger signage on electrical panels, documented lockout/tagout procedures, and availability of appropriate PPE for electrical maintenance staff are verified through periodic audits.

objective_elements.description: Electrical load distribution is managed to prevent overloading, with circuit breakers and protective devices properly rated and maintained.

measurable_elements.description: Electrical load distribution plans are documented, circuit breakers are properly rated, and periodic thermography scans of panels and switchgear identify potential overload or failure points.

objective_elements.description: Medical electrical equipment in patient care areas undergoes periodic electrical safety testing (leakage current, insulation resistance) per standards.

measurable_elements.description: Electrical safety testing records for medical equipment (leakage current, ground continuity, insulation resistance) are maintained per IEC 62353 or equivalent standards.

standards.title: Building and Structural Safety
standards.description: The organization ensures structural integrity, accessibility, and safety of the physical environment for patients, staff, and visitors.

objective_elements.description: The building has valid occupancy and structural safety certificates from competent authorities, with periodic structural audits conducted.

measurable_elements.description: Valid building occupancy certificates, structural stability certificates, and seismic safety assessments (where applicable) are maintained and current.

objective_elements.description: The facility provides barrier-free access for persons with disabilities, including ramps, adapted restrooms, signage, and assistive features.

measurable_elements.description: Accessibility audit reports verify presence of wheelchair ramps, adapted toilets, tactile flooring, braille signage, and other barrier-free features per national accessibility standards.

objective_elements.description: Flooring, corridors, stairways, and patient care areas are maintained to minimize slip, trip, and fall hazards.

measurable_elements.description: Anti-slip flooring, handrails in corridors and stairways, adequate lighting, and prompt spill management protocols reduce fall hazards, verified through periodic safety rounds.

objective_elements.description: Adequate ventilation, temperature control, lighting, and noise management are maintained in patient care and work areas.

measurable_elements.description: Environmental monitoring records for temperature, humidity, air changes per hour, illumination levels, and noise measurements in clinical areas are maintained within prescribed standards.

objective_elements.description: Elevators and lifts are maintained with valid certificates, regular servicing, and emergency rescue procedures in place.

measurable_elements.description: Elevator operation permits are current, maintenance contracts with qualified vendors are active, service logs are updated, and emergency rescue protocols are displayed and tested.

objective_elements.description: The organization implements evidence-based healing environment design principles to enhance patient comfort and recovery outcomes.

measurable_elements.description: Design elements such as natural lighting, noise reduction, patient privacy considerations, wayfinding systems, and therapeutic environments are incorporated and assessed for patient experience impact.

standards.title: Waste Management and Hazardous Materials Safety
standards.description: The organization manages biomedical waste, hazardous materials, and chemical substances safely in compliance with regulatory requirements.

objective_elements.description: Biomedical waste is segregated, collected, stored, transported, and disposed of in accordance with national biomedical waste management regulations.

measurable_elements.description: Biomedical waste management follows color-coded segregation, designated storage areas with holding time limits, authorized transport, and documented disposal through licensed waste handlers.

objective_elements.description: The organization holds valid authorization for biomedical waste generation and maintains contracts with authorized waste treatment facilities.

measurable_elements.description: Valid biomedical waste authorization from the pollution control board, current contracts with CBWTF or equivalent licensed treatment facilities, and annual returns filing records are maintained.

objective_elements.description: Hazardous chemicals and materials are identified, inventoried, handled safely, and stored with appropriate safety data sheets accessible.

measurable_elements.description: A hazardous chemicals inventory is maintained with safety data sheets (SDS) accessible at point of use, compatible storage arrangements, and spill response kits available.

objective_elements.description: Staff handling biomedical waste and hazardous materials receive appropriate training, immunization, and personal protective equipment.

measurable_elements.description: Training records, immunization status (Hepatitis B, Tetanus), and PPE issuance logs for waste handling staff are documented and reviewed periodically.

objective_elements.description: Needle-stick injury and sharps injury prevention protocols are established with post-exposure prophylaxis procedures.

measurable_elements.description: Needle-stick and sharps injury reporting mechanisms, PEP protocols, injury tracking logs, and root cause analysis for recurring incidents are documented and monitored.

objective_elements.description: Radioactive materials and radiation-emitting equipment are managed per Atomic Energy Regulatory Board guidelines with proper licensing.

measurable_elements.description: AERB licenses for radiation equipment, radiation safety officer appointment, personnel dosimetry records, and area monitoring reports are current and compliant.

objective_elements.description: General waste (non-hazardous) including solid waste and liquid waste is managed per municipal regulations with recycling where feasible.

measurable_elements.description: General solid waste segregation (wet/dry/recyclable), sewage treatment plant or municipal connection compliance, and waste audit records are maintained with recycling metrics tracked.

standards.title: Emergency Preparedness and Disaster Management
standards.description: The organization has a comprehensive emergency and disaster management program to prepare for, respond to, and recover from internal and external emergencies.

objective_elements.description: A documented hospital disaster management plan (HDMP) addresses natural disasters, epidemics, mass casualty incidents, and internal emergencies.

measurable_elements.description: A comprehensive hospital disaster management plan covering multiple hazard scenarios (earthquake, flood, fire, epidemic, bomb threat, mass casualty) with command structure and resource mobilization is documented.

objective_elements.description: Mock disaster drills are conducted periodically, involving internal and external stakeholders, with documented evaluation and improvement plans.

measurable_elements.description: Mock disaster drills are conducted at least annually, with scenario-based exercises, multi-departmental participation, post-drill debriefing reports, and action plans for identified weaknesses.

objective_elements.description: A hospital incident command system (HICS) or equivalent framework is established to coordinate emergency response across all functions.

measurable_elements.description: An incident command structure with defined roles (incident commander, operations, logistics, planning, finance) is documented, with trained personnel assigned and contact details updated.

objective_elements.description: Emergency supplies, medications, equipment caches, and communication systems are pre-positioned and regularly checked for readiness.

measurable_elements.description: Emergency supply inventories (medications, consumables, equipment) are pre-positioned at designated locations, inspected quarterly for expiry and functionality, with replenishment protocols documented.

objective_elements.description: The organization has linkages with external emergency services, government disaster agencies, and mutual aid agreements with neighboring facilities.

measurable_elements.description: Documented memoranda of understanding with local emergency services (fire, police, ambulance), disaster management authorities, and neighboring hospitals for mutual aid during mass casualty events.

objective_elements.description: Business continuity plans ensure continued operation of critical clinical and support services during and after disruptive events.

measurable_elements.description: Business continuity plans for critical services (emergency, ICU, OT, pharmacy, laboratory, blood bank) address staffing, supply chain, IT systems, and alternate site arrangements during disruptions.

standards.title: Security, Utilities, and Essential Services
standards.description: The organization provides a safe and secure environment with reliable utilities including water, power, medical gases, and HVAC systems.

objective_elements.description: A comprehensive security program protects patients, staff, visitors, and property through access control, surveillance, and threat assessment.

measurable_elements.description: Security measures including access control systems, CCTV surveillance, visitor management protocols, and security personnel deployment are documented with incident response procedures.

objective_elements.description: Safe and adequate water supply is ensured through testing, treatment, storage, and distribution systems with backup provisions.

measurable_elements.description: Water quality testing reports (bacteriological and chemical), storage tank cleaning records, water treatment plant logs, and backup water supply arrangements are documented and reviewed.

objective_elements.description: Medical gas systems (oxygen, compressed air, vacuum, nitrous oxide) are safely installed, maintained, and monitored with alarm systems.

measurable_elements.description: Medical gas pipeline installation compliance certificates, manifold room safety measures, area alarm panels, and periodic pipeline integrity testing records are maintained.

objective_elements.description: HVAC systems in critical areas (OT, ICU, CSSD, laboratory) are maintained with appropriate air quality, pressure differentials, and filtration standards.

measurable_elements.description: HVAC maintenance records, air quality testing reports, HEPA filter change logs, pressure differential monitoring in OTs and clean rooms, and air changes per hour verification are documented.

objective_elements.description: Vulnerable patient populations (neonates, pediatric, psychiatric, elderly) are provided with additional security safeguards appropriate to their needs.

measurable_elements.description: Enhanced security protocols for vulnerable populations include infant tagging systems, restricted nursery access, psychiatric unit safety features, and fall prevention measures for elderly patients.

objective_elements.description: The organization implements smart building management systems integrating utility monitoring, energy efficiency tracking, and predictive maintenance capabilities.

measurable_elements.description: Integrated building management systems for energy monitoring, automated utility controls, predictive maintenance analytics, and sustainability dashboards are implemented with performance improvement trends.

standards.title: Medical Equipment Management Program
standards.description: The organization has a systematic equipment management program covering selection, procurement, installation, operation, maintenance, and disposal of medical devices.

objective_elements.description: A comprehensive medical equipment management plan is documented, covering the entire lifecycle from needs assessment through procurement to disposal.

measurable_elements.description: A documented equipment management plan covers needs assessment, procurement criteria, installation protocols, user training, preventive maintenance schedules, and end-of-life disposal procedures.

objective_elements.description: A master equipment inventory is maintained with unique identifiers, locations, maintenance status, and calibration tracking for all medical devices.

measurable_elements.description: An equipment asset register with unique IDs, serial numbers, locations, commission dates, warranty status, AMC details, and maintenance history is maintained and updated in real-time.

objective_elements.description: Preventive and corrective maintenance is performed per manufacturer recommendations and documented schedules with downtime tracking.

measurable_elements.description: Preventive maintenance schedules per manufacturer guidelines, corrective maintenance work orders, equipment downtime logs, and mean time between failure analysis are documented and reviewed.

objective_elements.description: Critical medical equipment is calibrated by accredited agencies at defined intervals with traceability to national or international standards.

measurable_elements.description: Calibration certificates from NABL-accredited or equivalent agencies for critical equipment (monitors, ventilators, defibrillators, infusion pumps, analyzers) are maintained with traceability records.

objective_elements.description: The organization implements advanced equipment analytics including usage optimization, technology assessment, and evidence-based replacement planning.

measurable_elements.description: Equipment utilization analytics, health technology assessments for new acquisitions, total cost of ownership analysis, and evidence-based replacement priority matrices are used for capital planning decisions.
